summary: Red-gate proposal for labeling a new v9 mini-set: strict first-run claims require a frozen, uninspected, non-target-derived cohort; local source-inspected or prior-failure-derived sets should be labeled diagnostic-only unless a truly untouched source pool and pre-run freeze are documented.

first_run_validation_rules: The question file and any manifest must be frozen before the first model run, before answer inspection, before retrieval-chunk inspection, and before scorer/guard edits based on the mini-set.
The cohort must not be built from known failed cases, high-latency tails, scorer bugs, or target projects selected because prior loops exposed a weakness.
The tested code path must not contain qid-, target-title-, target-org-, answer-value-, or exact source-snippet-specific fixes for the new set.
The registry entry must exist before aggregation, and the first-run prefix should be whitelisted by the aggregator before any scoreboard claim is allowed.

Only the first execution can support the first-run claim; after answers, retrieval traces, failure causes, or judge outputs are inspected, later same-set reruns become exposed regression or diagnostic evidence.
diagnostic_only_rules: Use diagnostic-only for probes, no-judge rows, field-score-only rows, latency sweeps, scorer calibration, targeted failure probes, target-fix checks, false-positive checks, and any set designed after inspecting prior failure families.
Use diagnostic-only when prompts are new but source projects are already exposed, or when the set is generated from local source inspection over the already-used corpus.
Use diagnostic-only for L112-style follow-up on the same eight-case non-qv8 cohort; the useful signals are false abstention, field_score issues, latency, and cost, not a new comparable EDD score.
development_only_rules: Use development-only for drafts, fixture-design files, target-assignment drafts, ungrounded question ideas, or any set whose expected labels or source evidence were inspected while guard/scorer code was still being changed.
Unknown or unregistered files must remain non-promotable, consistent with the existing do_not_promote_until_reviewed default.

rules: Keep expected answers, field labels, source snippets, and scorer-only markers out of prompts and model-visible retrieval context.

Do not add aliases, guard markers, scorer exceptions, retrieval boosts, or post-trim exceptions keyed to v9 qids, exact target titles, target organizations, known answer values, or copied local-source wording.
Do not inspect v9 candidate answers, retrieved chunks, failure causes, or judge outputs until the frozen first run has completed and the run label has been recorded.
Do not promote a fix validated only on the same v9 failures; require generalized fixtures plus a separate untouched cohort or explicitly label the rerun as diagnostic repair evidence.

For mixed answerable/unavailable/private-contact questions, preserve field-level scoring outside aggregate EDD until the scorer is reviewed; do not let a clean aggregate score hide over-refusal or unsafe exposure.

accepted_fields_or_changes.wording: This v9 mini-set was generated from local corpus/source inspection. It is prompt-fresh but source-inspected, so it must not be described as blind, unseen-source, or strict held-out validation unless a separate untouched source pool and pre-run freeze are documented. Report results as source-inspected first-execution diagnostic evidence; after answer/retrieval/failure inspection, all reruns are exposed regression or diagnostic-only. It does not replace the strict scoreboard row, and no-judge or field-score-only EDD values are not comparable with judged EDD rows.

preferred_if_generated_from_local_source.reason: Prompt-fresh mini-set generated from local corpus/source inspection after prior v7/v8/L108-L112 loop insights; useful for guard, field-score, and latency evidence but not strict held-out validation.

allowed_only_if_untouched_source_and_fre.reason: Frozen before execution from an untouched source/target pool, with no answer, retrieval, failure, or target-specific code inspection before the first run; only the first execution may support validation and later reruns are exposed regression.

if_used_for_target_fix_or_latency_after_.reason: Built or selected to measure known guard, field-score, or latency risks after L112; measures repair/generalization signals only and must not enter the strict scoreboard.

rules: Reject promotion if any registry status is unknown_needs_review or any claim_use starts with do_not_promote.
Reject strict promotion if the mini-set came from already-exposed source projects without explicit source-inspected caveat language.
Reject strict promotion if the report calls a no-judge EDD comparable to judged EDD, or if field-score-only rows are folded into aggregate EDD.

Allow strict scoreboard promotion only when registry claim_use is first_run_and_regression_only, the run label is the documented first-run prefix, judged quality metrics are present, and the report preserves the one-time nature of the claim.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Calling a local-source-inspected v9 mini-set strict fresh validation without caveats
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Conflicts with the v7/v8/L108-L112 doctrine that source-exposed or prior-insight-derived cohorts are diagnostic-only.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Using L112 no-judge EDD or v9 no-judge EDD as a comparable leaderboard score
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The L108-L112 report states no-judge EDD is not comparable because groundedness/relevance are null.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Adding v9-specific guard/scorer exceptions after reading first-run failures and then reporting the same-set rerun as validation
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: This is target-fix contamination and should be labeled diagnostic repair evidence only.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Inventing a new source-inspected first-run claim_use that the aggregator treats as scoreboard without an explicit code review
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Existing promotion logic recognizes first_run_and_regression_only; new claim_use names should default diagnostic unless the orchestrator updates and verifies aggregation intentionally.

needs_orchestrator_review: Confirm whether v9 target projects come from a truly untouched source pool or only from local inspection of the already-exposed corpus.
Confirm whether the v9 generator saw exact answer values, source snippets, expected field labels, previous failure notes, or L112/v8 target-fix candidates before the first run.
If strict first-run promotion is desired, add an explicit first-run prefix mapping and verify the aggregate keeps all non-first reruns diagnostic-only.
Decide whether the final report should use the stricter wording source-inspected diagnostic mini-set or the narrower source-inspected first-execution evidence label.

commands_run: Get-ChildItem I:/0706/rfp-rag-project and requested worker_outputs folder
rg -n for first-run/diagnostic/development/claim_use/exposure/v8/v9/leakage/contamination/target terms across requested inputs
observed_results: Output folder existed and was empty before writing this proposal.

Exposure registry rules state strict validation requires a frozen, uninspected first run; inspected failures plus targeted fixes spend holdout value; probes/targeted/no-judge sets are diagnostic; drafts become exposed after answer/retrieval/failure inspection.
Existing registry labels v7 source-exposed, v8 semi-fresh/source-exposed, L106 draft, and L108/L109 grounded guard sets as diagnostic_only.

Experiment log L80-L112 repeatedly keeps unknown/unregistered, source-exposed, no-judge, targeted, and prior-failure-derived rows out of the scoreboard; L112 is the best diagnostic row but not strict validation.
The L108-L112 report explicitly says the cohort is diagnostic-only, no-judge EDD is not comparable, and further work should use a new untouched mini-set or latency/cost sweep.
JSON parse validation passed for this contract file.
